questions.question: Which attribute specifies the primary language of an HTML document or element?
questions.explanation: The lang attribute declares the language of the element's content using a BCP 47 language tag (e.g., lang="en", lang="fr"). It is typically set on the <html> element for the entire page.

questions.question: What does the dir attribute control?
choices: The directory where files are stored
The display order of child elements
The reading level of the content
questions.explanation: The dir attribute sets the text directionality. It accepts ltr (left-to-right), rtl (right-to-left), or auto (let the browser determine direction from the content).

questions.question: What is the purpose of the hreflang attribute on an <a> or <link> element?
choices: It translates the linked page automatically
It specifies the language of the linked resource
It changes the display language of the current page
It sets the encoding of the linked document
questions.explanation: The hreflang attribute indicates the language of the document at the linked URL. It helps search engines and browsers understand which language version of a page to serve.

questions.question: Which attribute indicates whether the content of an element should be translated by automated translation services?
questions.explanation: The translate attribute accepts yes or no values. Setting translate="no" tells translation tools to leave the content as-is, which is useful for brand names, code, or technical terms.

questions.question: To set a document's text direction to right-to-left, you use dir="___".
questions.explanation: The value rtl (right-to-left) is used for languages such as Arabic and Hebrew that are written from right to left.

questions.question: The language code for English in the lang attribute is ___.
questions.explanation: The BCP 47 language tag for English is "en". More specific variants include "en-US" for American English and "en-GB" for British English.
